name: Nebius Group N.V.

profile.description: Nebius Group N.V. is a full-stack AI infrastructure company building large-scale GPU clusters, cloud platforms, and developer tools for the global AI industry. Formerly known as Yandex N.V. (Russia's largest search engine), the company completed a corporate restructuring in 2024, severing all Russian ties and redomiciling to the Netherlands. Key subsidiaries include TripleTen (edtech) and Avride (autonomous driving). Strategic partnership with NVIDIA including a $2B investment. Headquartered in Schiphol, Netherlands.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Nebius executed one of the most dramatic corporate transformations in recent history -- divesting its entire Russian internet business (Yandex) and rebuilding as a pure-play AI infrastructure company, growing revenue from $9.8M in 2023 to $529.8M in 2025 to $399M in Q1 2026 alone (684% YoY growth) [1][2]. This is a complete business model reinvention at extraordinary speed. AI Deployment (2/2): AI infrastructure IS the product -- Nebius builds and operates large-scale GPU clusters, AI cloud platforms, and inference services (Token Factory) that directly generate revenue. Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA of $129.5M (up from a $53.7M loss a year ago) with 45% EBITDA margin in the core AI cloud division demonstrates AI revenue at scale [1]. Market Share (1/2): Rapidly gaining share in the emerging AI cloud infrastructure market but not yet a category leader; NVIDIA's $2B investment and Meta infrastructure agreement validate Nebius's technical differentiation, but it competes against hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) with far larger scale [2]. Product Pipeline (2/2): Specific and visible pipeline -- contracted power capacity exceeds 3.5GW (raised to 4GW+ by end 2026), gigawatt-scale AI factory under construction in Missouri, 1.2GW Pennsylvania site secured, full-year 2026 guidance of $3-3.4B revenue [1][2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Deep engineering DNA inherited from Yandex's 30+ years of search and ML infrastructure, with 1,543 employees predominantly in engineering roles [2].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): CEO Arkady Volozh's vision -- 'Nebius has been built for AI since day one, not adapted from a general-purpose cloud, but designed for what developers actually need' -- is clear and specific to the company's technical differentiation, but it describes a product positioning rather than a transformative purpose beyond profit [2]. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of charitable giving or community programs; the company is in hyper-growth mode focused entirely on infrastructure buildout [3]. Employee Retention (1/2): With only 1,543 employees, the company maintains a lean, technically elite team inherited from Yandex's deep engineering culture; leadership stability through the dramatic corporate transformation suggests strong internal conviction [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): Founder Arkady Volozh retains 52% voting power and approximately 11% economic interest via Class B shares through a family trust, demonstrating sustained alignment. NVIDIA's $2B strategic investment provides extraordinary external validation of the long-term vision [2][3].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics with extraordinary growth profile. Revenue growth of 684% YoY in Q1 2026 ($399M vs $50.9M) and 5-year revenue CAGR exceeding 250% are among the highest in the public markets [1]. Profit margin of 93.1% reflects one-time gains but the core AI cloud division achieved 45% adjusted EBITDA margin, nearly doubling sequentially [1]. PEG ratio of 0.63 against massive revenue growth is attractive. Debt-to-equity of 132.4% reflects $9.6B debt against $9.4B cash from recent capital raises [1][3]. Key weakness is the extreme Forward P/E of 639.7x based on forward EPS of $0.36, reflecting a company in early monetization with massive capex ($16-20B guided for 2026) [1]. ROE of 14.1% is near the 15% target. Liquidity is robust at $4.06B daily trading value.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Nebius IS AI infrastructure -- the company builds and operates the GPU clusters, cloud platforms, and inference services that the global AI industry runs on [1][2]. AI disruption risk is definitionally zero because AI growth IS the company's business growth. Contracted power capacity of 3.5GW+ with 75% owned (not leased) creates physical infrastructure moats that cannot be replicated by software alone. The NVIDIA strategic alliance ($2B investment) and Meta infrastructure agreement provide long-term demand visibility [2]. The primary risk is not AI disruption but competition from hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) with greater scale and existing customer relationships. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI revenue is the entire business. Q1 2026 revenue of $399M is 100% AI-derived, with full-year 2026 guidance of $3-3.4B, inference (Token Factory) is the fastest-growing segment, and the company has clear revenue visibility through contracted capacity [1].

infinite_game.summary: Nebius demonstrates significant infinite game characteristics driven by its extraordinary willingness to transform -- abandoning Yandex's entire Russian internet empire to rebuild as an AI infrastructure company is one of the most dramatic existential flexibility examples in modern corporate history. Founder Arkady Volozh's 35+ year leadership continuity through multiple eras (search, cloud, AI) provides strong Just Cause foundations. However, the company is too young in its current form to have proven long-term consistency, and governance concentration raises questions about organizational resilience beyond the founder.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Nebius has a genuine cause in democratizing AI infrastructure -- making enterprise-grade GPU compute accessible beyond the hyperscaler oligopoly. Volozh's 35+ year career building technology infrastructure (from Yandex search to AI cloud) shows consistent commitment to enabling developers, and the decision to abandon Yandex's profitable Russian business rather than compromise on global AI ambitions demonstrates purpose beyond immediate profit [2][3]. However, the company is only two years into its current form, and the cause has not yet been tested through multiple market cycles.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The fact that 1,543 employees chose to follow Nebius through the Yandex corporate breakup and complete business model reinvention suggests meaningful organizational trust [2]. The lean engineering-first culture inherited from Yandex's deep technical DNA provides a foundation of shared competence. However, the company is too young and too small to have extensive organizational trust data, and the rapid scaling from 1,500 to potentially thousands of employees introduces culture risk.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Nebius explicitly positions itself as complementary to, not competing against, hyperscalers -- the NVIDIA partnership and Meta agreement show the company uses larger players' strengths to build its own capabilities rather than trying to destroy them [2]. CEO Volozh frames competition as 'designed for what developers actually need' rather than 'beating AWS,' demonstrating an infinite competitive mindset focused on self-improvement.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): Nebius executed one of the most dramatic corporate transformations in modern history -- divesting its entire profitable Russian internet business (Yandex, Russia's #1 search engine), redomiciling from Russia to the Netherlands, and rebuilding from scratch as an AI infrastructure company [2][3]. This was a voluntary abandonment of a dominant, profitable business in service of a larger ambition, directly analogous to Apple's Mac-to-iPhone transition that Sinek cites as the canonical example of existential flexibility.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Volozh's decision to divest Yandex and rebuild Nebius required genuine courage -- walking away from billions in Russian revenue with no guarantee the AI infrastructure bet would succeed [2][3]. The $16-20B capex commitment for 2026 represents an enormous bet on AI infrastructure demand. However, the courage was partly forced by geopolitical circumstances (Russia-Ukraine war, sanctions risk), making it difficult to separate voluntary principle from external necessity.

investment_thesis: Nebius is the highest-growth public AI infrastructure pure-play, delivering Q1 2026 revenue of $399M (684% YoY growth) with AI cloud EBITDA margin of 45% and full-year 2026 guidance of $3-3.4B revenue at 40% adjusted EBITDA margin [1]. The NVIDIA strategic partnership ($2B investment) and Meta infrastructure agreement provide demand visibility, while 3.5GW+ contracted power capacity (75% owned) creates physical infrastructure moats. PEG ratio of 0.63 against triple-digit revenue growth is among the most attractive growth valuations in the market. The transition from Yandex to Nebius represents a rare complete corporate reinvention executed at extraordinary speed.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on extreme valuation and execution risk. Forward P/E of 639.7x reflects a company that is massively investing ahead of earnings, with $16-20B in guided 2026 capex creating negative free cash flow of -$6.1B [1]. Debt-to-equity of 132.4% and total debt of $9.6B against $9.4B cash leave thin liquidity margins if AI infrastructure demand disappoints. Governance risk is elevated -- Volozh's 52% voting control via Class B shares allows 'Controlled Company' exemptions from Nasdaq governance requirements, and material weaknesses in internal controls over fixed assets and TripleTen revenue recognition remain unremediated as of year-end 2025 [3]. The company competes against hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) with vastly greater resources, and any AI infrastructure demand slowdown would expose the levered balance sheet.
key_catalysts: Full-year 2026 revenue guidance of $3-3.4B with 40% EBITDA margin represents 5-6x YoY growth if executed, potentially driving multiple expansion as profitability proves sustainable at scale

Gigawatt-scale AI factory construction in Missouri and 1.2GW Pennsylvania site provide visible capacity expansion path to 4GW+ by year-end 2026, with NVIDIA partnership enabling early adoption of next-gen GPU architectures

Inference segment (Token Factory) is the fastest-growing product line, positioning Nebius for the shift from AI training to production inference workloads that represents the next phase of AI monetization
key_risks: Forward P/E of 639.7x and -$6.1B free cash flow reflect a company investing far ahead of earnings -- any slowdown in AI infrastructure demand or capex overrun could severely pressure the stock given the levered balance sheet ($9.6B debt)

Governance concentration with founder Volozh holding 52% voting power and unremediated material weaknesses in internal controls create investor protection gaps that could trigger institutional selling

Competition from hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) with greater scale, existing customer relationships, and deeper balance sheets could compress margins and limit Nebius's addressable market as AI infrastructure commoditizes
sources: Nebius Group Q1 2026 Earnings Release and FY2026 Revenue Guidance -- SEC Form 6-K, May 2026
NVIDIA $2B Investment in Nebius, Meta Infrastructure Agreement, and CEO Volozh Commentary -- NVIDIA Newsroom, CNBC, Futurum Group, March 2026
Nebius Group Governance, Material Weaknesses, and Yandex Transition -- SEC Form 20-F, AInvest, Wikipedia, 2025-2026
Yahoo Finance -- NBIS Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed May 2026
